Control Arm - N.evidence: In the '2. Patients and methods' section, it states: 'From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm.' The ADT arm represents the control arm.

Control Arm.evidence: The title of the article states 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer'. The abstract also mentions 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients', clearly indicating 'ADT alone' as the comparison group that does not receive the experimental treatment (ADT plus Docetaxel).

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'Abstract' section states: 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients'. This indicates two treatment arms: 'ADT plus D' and 'ADT alone'. The 'Patients and methods' section further confirms this: 'Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone'.

Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The title of the article states 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone'. The abstract further clarifies 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone'. In the 'Patients and methods' section, it is explicitly stated: 'Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone'.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) plus Docetaxel (D) (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles)
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The 'Patients and methods' section under '2. Patients and methods' states: 'Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone.' This describes the experimental treatment arm (Treatment Arm 1).

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: The 'Design, setting, and participants' section states: 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients'. Further details are provided in '2. Patients and methods': 'From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm.' The experimental arm is 'ADT plus D', which had 192 participants.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: The 'Abstract' section states: 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients; median follow-up of 7 yr.' and then 'After a median follow-up of 83.9 mo, median OS in the overall population was 62.1 mo'. The 'Results' section under '3.1. Overall survival' also confirms: 'After a median follow-up of 83.9 mo (95% CI, 82.9–84.7), 242 patients had died'. Additionally, Table 2, row 'Median follow-up, mo' for 'GETUG-AFU 15' shows '83.9'.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.value: Chemotherapy (Docetaxel) and Hormonal Therapy (Androgen Deprivation Therapy)
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The title of the article states 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone'. The 'Patients and methods' section (f5fb17a8-9577-4f18-8f8b-adad1a4a8a7f) further specifies 'Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone. ADT consisted of orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors.' Docetaxel is a chemotherapy agent, and Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) is a form of hormonal therapy.

Total Participants - N.evidence: The 'Abstract' section states: 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients'. The 'Patients and methods' section further confirms: 'From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm'. The 'Results' section also reiterates: 'The GETUG-AFU15 trial randomized 385 patients including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm'.

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document, which reports on the GETUG-AFU15 trial, does not mention any assessment of participants' overall well-being, satisfaction with health status, or perceived quality of life using a standardized questionnaire or survey during the clinical trial. While reference [26] mentions "quality of life" in the context of the GETUG 12 trial, this is a different study and not relevant to the primary focus of this document.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The document details the primary and secondary endpoints as overall survival (OS), biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS), and radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS) in the Abstract and Section 2.1 'Statistics'. The Results (Section 3) and Discussion (Section 4) also focus exclusively on these survival and progression outcomes. There is no mention of quality of life as an outcome measure or any reported results related to it for the GETUG-AFU15 trial. While reference [26] mentions 'quality of life' in the context of the GETUG 12 trial, this document is specifically about the GETUG-AFU15 trial.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: The 'ADT plus D' arm is the treatment arm, and it included 192 patients (Table 1). The study design states that eligible patients were randomized to 'ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles)', indicating that all patients in this arm received docetaxel as part of their assigned treatment.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: In Section 3.2, it is stated: 'In the ADT arm, 127 of 149 patients (85%) treated for progressive disease received D'. The total number of patients in the ADT (control) arm was 193 (Table 1). Therefore, 127 out of 193 patients in the control arm received docetaxel as a subsequent treatment during the trial, which calculates to (127/193) * 100 = 65.8%.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The abstract mentions 'For upfront metastatic patients, OS was 52.6 mo (95% CI, 43.3–66.8) and 41.5 mo (95% CI, 36.3–54.5), respectively (HR: 0.93 [95% CI, 0.69–1.25]; p = 0.6)'. Section 3.1 'Overall survival' further states 'Patients with metastatic disease after failure of local treatment (28%) had a significantly longer median OS than those with metastases at diagnosis (72%) at 83.1 mo (95% CI, 68.6–NR) versus 46.5 mo (95% CI, 40.5–54.6) (HR: 1.57 [95% CI, 1.09–2.26]; p = 0.015)'. 'Metastases at diagnosis' and 'upfront metastatic patients' refer to synchronous metastases.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: Section 3.1 'Overall survival' states 'Patients with metastatic disease after failure of local treatment (28%) had a significantly longer median OS than those with metastases at diagnosis (72%) at 83.1 mo (95% CI, 68.6–NR) versus 46.5 mo (95% CI, 40.5–54.6) (HR: 1.57 [95% CI, 1.09–2.26]; p = 0.015)'. 'Metastatic disease after failure of local treatment' refers to metachronous disease.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The abstract explicitly states 'Median OS in ADT plus D and ADT arms, respectively, was for HVD patients: 39.8 mo (95% CI, 28.0–53.4) versus 35.1 mo (95% CI, 29.9–43.6) (HR: 0.78 [95% CI, 0.56–1.09]; p = 0.14)'. The study also defines HVD in Section 2 'Patients and methods' and reports results for HVD in Section 3 'Results' and Table 2.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The abstract explicitly states 'for low-volume disease (LVD) patients; median was not reached (NR; 95% CI, 69.5–NR) and 83.4 mo (95% CI, 61.8–NR) (HR: 1.02 [95% CI, 0.67–1.55]; p = 0.9)'. The study also defines LVD in Section 2 'Patients and methods' and reports results for LVD in Section 3 'Results' and Table 2.

TTPSA (mo) | Treatment.evidence: In section 3.2, under 'Biochemical and radiographic progression-free survival', it states: 'The bPFS was significantly longer for patients randomized in the ADT plus D arm in the overall population at 22.9 mo (95% CI, 19.5–28.4)'. bPFS (biochemical progression-free survival) is equivalent to TTPSA (Time To Progression of Serum Androgen).

TTPSA (mo) | Control.evidence: In section 3.2, under 'Biochemical and radiographic progression-free survival', it states: 'The bPFS was significantly longer for patients randomized in the ADT plus D arm in the overall population at 22.9 mo (95% CI, 19.5–28.4) versus 12.9 mo (95% CI, 11.9–17.7) (HR: 0.67 [95% CI, 0.54–0.84]; p < 0.001)'. The 12.9 mo refers to the ADT arm (control). bPFS (biochemical progression-free survival) is equivalent to TTPSA (Time To Progression of Serum Androgen).

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: The document mentions 'Four treatment-related deaths occurred during the course of chemotherapy in the ADT plus D arm', which are Grade 5 events. However, it does not provide the total number and percentage of all treatment-related adverse events with a grade of 3 or higher.
The document does not report the number and percentage of patients experiencing treatment-related adverse events with a grade of 3 or higher in the ADT alone (control) arm.

The 'Discussion' section states: 'Four treatment-related deaths occurred during the course of chemotherapy in the ADT plus D arm'. The total number of patients in the ADT plus D arm is 192 (from Table 1). Therefore, 4/192 = 2.08%.
The document only mentions treatment-related deaths in the ADT plus D arm and does not report any treatment-related Grade 5 adverse events (deaths) in the ADT alone (control) arm.

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The title of the article is 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer'. The abstract also states 'Survival benefits of docetaxel (D) added to androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT)' and 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone'. In the 'Patients and methods' section, it specifies 'Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone', clearly indicating Docetaxel as the add-on treatment to ADT.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document is a research paper detailing a specific randomized controlled trial (GETUG-AFU15) and comparing its results with other similar trials (CHAARTED, STAMPEDE). It discusses the methodology, results, and limitations of these specific trials, including issues like lack of statistical power for subgroup analyses. However, it does not provide a general 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) in randomized controlled trials (RCT)' as a broad statement or a meta-assessment of the quality of evidence across RCTs in general. The focus is on the specific findings and limitations of the trials discussed, not a general judgment on COE.

Treatment Class.value: Hormonal therapy (Androgen Deprivation Therapy) and Chemohormonal therapy (Androgen Deprivation Therapy plus Docetaxel)
Treatment Class.evidence: The title of the article is 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer'. The abstract and methods section describe the two treatment arms as 'ADT alone' and 'ADT plus D'. The discussion section also refers to 'chemohormonal therapy versus hormonal therapy' when comparing with similar trials (e.g., CHAARTED trial, section 4, paragraph 2).

Type of Therapy.value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel (D) versus ADT Alone. ADT consisted of orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors. Docetaxel was administered at 75 mg/m² for 21 days, up to nine cycles.
